expectations.evidence: Axes are demand durability and the co-teacher's departure; sign/decline/negotiate appear only as strategies scored in Q9. Q3 explicitly demotes a landlord-flexibility candidate to the ask list because it's resolvable before Friday — the resolvable-as-axis trap avoided by name.
expectations.text: axes-are-external-unknowns: branching happens on demand durability and the co-teacher's move — never on the lease decision itself

expectations.evidence: 'How many months of the combined rent (current unit + new unit) could you cover from savings if bookings dropped back to pre-feature levels?' — a number, not an adequacy confession; five more catalogued questions with the same shape.
expectations.text: elicits-missing-facts: rent, savings, or comparable financials appear as questions to the asker inside the response

expectations.evidence: Waitlist surviving into the term after this one, and the partner's accepted offer / confirmed start date. Both explicitly flagged as firing after Friday, so the signature must precede them.
expectations.text: watchpoint-observable: at least one watchpoint names a checkable event (fall enrollment numbers, the partner's job decision, a date)

expectations.evidence: Q1 through Q9 each surface as a bold question before their answer, including Q6's tile-coverage check and Q9's three-way strategy scoring.
expectations.text: process-visible: chain questions appear in the response before their answers

expectations.evidence: Ends on 'take the space, but don't take the vanilla version of the offer' — negotiate a break clause or sublease right before Friday, with a named fallback rule (cash-cushion threshold) if the landlord refuses. Recap table sits between the told futures and this commitment, per spec.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-and-committed: the reply ends on a committed move — likely deadline negotiation or a shorter term — never a bare 'it depends'
